title: Random Matrices as a model of Quantum Hydrodynamics
speaker: Paul Wiegman

proof_stages.label: Vortex-fluid problem and random-matrix motivation
proof_stages.summary: The talk reduces two-dimensional incompressible inviscid motion to vorticity, separates fast fluid motion from slow vortex-center motion, and poses the hydrodynamics of the resulting vortex fluid, motivated by the statistical analogy with complex random-matrix eigenvalues.

proof_stages.label: Anomalous Euler stress as target structure
proof_stages.summary: Ordinary incompressible Euler dynamics is modified by an anomalous pseudotensor stress whose divergence gives conservative forces normal to shear, and the main claim is that vortex-fluid hydrodynamics contains precisely such forces.

proof_stages.label: Euler vorticity equation and Kirchhoff point vortices
proof_stages.summary: Taking the curl of Euler’s equation gives frozen-in vorticity, and the point-vortex meromorphic ansatz reduces this continuum equation to finite-dimensional Kirchhoff equations for vortex positions with conserved circulations.

proof_stages.label: Chiral Hamiltonian vortex ensemble and Dyson gas
proof_stages.summary: Restricting to a separated same-sign vortex cluster and passing to a rotating frame gives Hamiltonian Kirchhoff dynamics whose stochastic Onsager equilibrium is the beta Dyson/Ginibre-type ensemble for planar eigenvalues.

proof_stages.label: Continuum density, flux, velocity, and stress tensor
proof_stages.summary: A large-vortex-number local-equilibrium limit is postulated, defining smooth vortex density, flux, and Eulerian velocity, and the holomorphic stress tensor is identified as the object whose anomalous correction must be computed.

proof_stages.label: Pole-identity computation of the anomaly
proof_stages.summary: Using the Kirchhoff velocities and a meromorphic Cauchy-type identity, the self-singular fast motion is subtracted, yielding the anomalous relation between fast and slow velocities, the anomalous Hamiltonian, and a modified Poisson/Virasoro-type hydrodynamic algebra.

proof_stages.label: Curved-space and Kähler random-matrix extension
proof_stages.summary: The anomalous stress is extended to curved surfaces, producing Liouville/gravitational trace anomaly terms and suggesting a covariant random-matrix measure on Kähler manifolds and random metrics.
